description_long: This gate consists of two thick, dark brown horizontal wooden beams supported by two vertical wooden posts. The posts are anchored into large, gray, rectangular concrete bases, giving the structure a sturdy and robust appearance. The horizontal beams are slightly offset and uneven, contributing to a rustic or utilitarian aesthetic. There are no visible hinges, handles, or decorative elements, suggesting a simple, functional design. The wood appears solid and untreated, while the concrete bases are smooth and angular. This type of gate is commonly used as a barrier or demarcation in parks, rural roads, or urban green spaces.
description: A simple, rustic wooden gate with two horizontal beams and two vertical posts anchored in large gray concrete bases.
description_view_0: Front view showing both horizontal wooden beams and the full width of the concrete bases.
description_view_1: Angled rear view highlighting the offset alignment of the beams and the depth of the concrete bases.
description_view_2: Back view with a clear look at the rear faces of the posts and beams, showing the same structure as the front.
description_view_3: Angled rear view from the opposite side, again emphasizing the staggered beams and the solid bases.
